text: Subscribe to SonicOctave's Kids Channel and press the bell icon to watch new Cartoon videos. The name of the story is Power of Habit. Once upon a time, there was a potter. He had many donkeys. He used to take all those donkeys to bring soil every day from the morning until the evening. And then he would make pots out of that soil. On his way back, he used to take rest under a big tree. He used to tie all the donkeys to the trunk of the tree and then go to sleep for a while. One day, when he was coming back and as usual wanted to tie all his donkey to the trunk of the tree, he realized that he is one rope short to tie all donkeys to the tree. So, he decided to take the donkey with him and keep an eye on him while he is resting. But unfortunately, donkey made so much of noise that potter could not take enough sleep. By then, there was one villager who was passing by. He saw that the potter is trying to take some rest but he could not because of the noises that the donkey made. So, he went to that potter and said to him, Oh dear friend, I'll give you a trick. Just take the donkey to his normal place and just pretend to tie a knot around him and around the tree. The potter did not really believe the villager's words. But then he thought of giving it a try. So, he took the donkey near to the trunk and pretended to tie a knot around donkey's neck and to the tree. He realized that the donkey was not moving. So, he went back and slept for a while. After a while, he woke up and wanted to take all his donkeys back to his home. He untied all the donkeys and started walking. When he looked at the tree, he realized that the donkey was not moving. When he looked back, he saw that the donkey that he pretended to tie to the tree was not moving at all. So, he went to the donkey and tried to take him away from that tree. But that donkey would not move. Coincidentally, the villager who gave this trick earlier was passing from there. As he saw the struggle of the potter, he went to him and said,

Oh my friend, now that you pretended to tie him down and it has worked, now you have to pretend to untie the donkey. If you don't do that, he would not move from there. So, the potter again pretended to untie the donkey and that worked like a magic. That donkey started walking with that potter again with all the other donkeys. So, the moral of the story is we are all tied by the habits. So, if you want to inculcate good virtues in yourself, try to make them a habit. Once it becomes a habit, it becomes a part of your character. Thank you for watching.
